button.setup.description: Text in button for performing setup

button.start.description: Text in button for starting an operation

button.copy.description: Text in button for copying content

button.copied.description: Text in button indicating content has been copied

button.restart.description: Text in button for regenerating content

button.preview.description: Text in button to open a preview of a file via URL

button.seeRawImage.description: Text in button for viewing the original image

button.seeRawVideo.description: Text in button for viewing the original video

button.edit.description: Text in button for editing content

button.login.description: Text in button for logging in

button.logout.description: Text in button for logging out

button.new.description: Text in button for creating a new item

button.delete.description: Text in button for deleting content

button.confirm.description: Text in button for confirming an action

button.cancel.description: Text in button for canceling an action

button.close.description: Text in button for closing a dialog or panel

button.expand.description: Tooltip text for the button that opens a fullscreen editor for a prompt textarea

button.alert.description: Text in button for issuing an alert

button.info.description: Text in button for displaying information

button.publish.description: Text in button for publishing content

button.watch.description: Text in button for watching content

button.verify.description: Text in button for user real name verification

button.profile.description: Text in button for displaying user profile

button.console.description: Text in button for displaying the console page to control content on the webpage

button.learn.description: Text in button for allowing users to learn content

button.buy.description: Text in button for allowing users to purchase content

button.try.description: Text in button for allowing users to try content

button.startForFree.description: Text in button for allowing users to start for free

button.download.description: Text in button for downloading files (e.g., audio, video, images)

button.downloadAndroid.description: Text in button for downloading the Android installation package

button.downloadIos.description: Text in button for downloading the iOS installation package

button.downloadWindows.description: Text in button for downloading the Windows desktop installer

button.downloadMac.description: Text in button for downloading the macOS desktop installer

button.installIos.description: Text in button for installing the iOS app

button.apply.description: Text in button for allowing users to apply for API services

button.refresh.description: Text in button for refreshing the page

button.pay.description: Text in button for allowing users to pay for services

button.repay.description: Text in button for allowing users to continue payment for services

button.applied.description: Text in button indicating the user has applied for the service

button.acquire.description: Text in button for allowing users to acquire API service tokens or keys

button.fillExample.description: Text in button for allowing users to fill in example data

button.buyMore.description: Text in button for allowing users to top up. Must translate to 'Top Up'.

button.update.description: Text in button for updating content

button.view.description: Text in button for viewing content

button.create.description: Text in button for creating content

button.referralEarnings.description: Text in the button that displays referral earnings, indicating that users can earn money by inviting others to use the service.

button.finish.description: Text in the button used to complete an operation

entity.id.description: The unique identifier for the entity

entity.website.description: Website name, no translation needed, keep as 'Ace Data Cloud'

entity.platform.description: Text indicating this is an API service platform

status.loading.description: Status text displayed while content is loading

error.appleSignInFailed.message: Sign in with Apple failed. Please try again or use another sign-in method.
error.appleSignInFailed.description: Error toast shown when native Sign in with Apple fails for a reason other than the user cancelling.

error.loginLinkExpired.message: Login link expired. Please sign in again.
error.loginLinkExpired.description: Shown on the desktop client when an OAuth callback is dropped (state mismatch or expired).

entity.createdAt.description: The time the entity was created

entity.updatedAt.description: The time the entity was updated

entity.order.description: The order of the entity, which includes monetary and service information that the user needs to pay for

message.noTasks.message: No historical tasks, please start a new task~
message.noTasks.description: Message when there are no tasks

message.guestTasks.message: Log in to start a task and view your creations here.
message.guestTasks.description: Prompt shown in the task list area for not-logged-in guests

message.usedAmount.description: Used to display the amount of service that has been used

message.mobileAppDescription.message: Take the full AceData studio everywhere — on Android, iPhone & iPad, Windows, and macOS. Install in one tap, or scan a QR code to get started.
message.mobileAppDescription.description: Description text for the app download page

message.mobileAndroidHint.message: For Android phones and tablets. Install from Google Play for automatic updates, or grab the APK directly.

message.mobileAndroidPending.message: The Android installation package is not ready yet, please try again later.
message.mobileAndroidPending.description: Hint when the Android installation package is not available

message.mobileAvailableNow.description: Used to display the status that Android is available for download

message.mobileSecureDelivery.description: Used to emphasize that the download link is delivered via CDN

message.mobileDirectInstall.description: Used to describe the Android side as a direct APK installation method

message.mobileLatestRelease.description: Used to indicate that the current display is the latest public version

message.mobileSharedAccount.message: Shared account and quota with the web version
message.mobileSharedAccount.description: Used to explain that the mobile version shares the account with the web version

message.mobileIosHint.message: For iPhone and iPad — install directly from the App Store, sharing your account and quota with the web app.

message.mobileIosPending.message: The iOS build is being finalized with Apple certificates and provisioning profiles, and will be available here shortly.
message.mobileIosPending.description: Hint when the iOS installation package is not available

message.mobileComingSoon.description: Short status label shown on a platform card (e.g. iOS) while its build is being prepared

message.mobileNowOnAppStore.description: Status label shown on the iOS card when the app is live on the App Store

message.desktopWindowsHint.message: For Windows 10 and later (64-bit). Download the installer and double-click to set up.

message.desktopMacHint.message: For macOS 11 and later. Choose the build that matches your Mac — Apple Silicon (M-series) or Intel.

message.desktopUnsignedNote.message: The desktop apps are unsigned beta builds. On Windows, choose ‘More info → Run anyway’ at the SmartScreen prompt; on macOS, right-click the app and choose ‘Open’ to bypass Gatekeeper.
message.desktopUnsignedNote.description: Note explaining the desktop builds are unsigned betas and how to open them

button.getOnGooglePlay.description: Button label that opens the app's Google Play Store listing

button.getOnAppStore.message: Get it on the App Store
button.getOnAppStore.description: Button label that opens the app's Apple App Store listing

message.mobileApkFallback.message: No access to Google Play? Download the APK directly.
message.mobileApkFallback.description: Shown next to a secondary button offering a direct APK download for users who cannot access Google Play

message.mobilePlayStoreHint.description: Hint under the QR code explaining that scanning it opens the Google Play listing

message.mobileAppStoreHint.message: Scan to open the App Store
message.mobileAppStoreHint.description: Hint under the QR code explaining that scanning it opens the App Store listing

message.mobileInstallNote.message: When installing the enterprise or Ad Hoc package for the first time on iOS, the system may require you to trust the certificate in 'Settings > General > VPN & Device Management'.

title.mobileApp.description: Title for the app download page

title.mobileFastAccess.message: Faster Access, No App Store Detours
title.mobileFastAccess.description: Title for the download page advantage card, emphasizing quick installation

title.mobileTrustedRelease.message: Centralized Version Management, More Stable Distribution
title.mobileTrustedRelease.description: Title for the download page advantage card, emphasizing version and distribution stability

title.mobileUnifiedExperience.message: Consistent Experience Across Mobile and Web
title.mobileUnifiedExperience.description: Title for the download page advantage card, emphasizing unified experience

nav.mobileApp.description: Navigation entry for the app download page (mobile + desktop)

message.mobileFastAccess.message: Users can scan the code on their phone for direct installation, or download the APK on the desktop and distribute it to team members, suitable for quick trials and internal testing.
message.mobileFastAccess.description: Download page advantage description, emphasizing quick access

message.mobileTrustedRelease.message: Download entries are centralized on one page, with version numbers, platform status, and installation methods visible at a glance, reducing communication costs caused by scattered links.
message.mobileTrustedRelease.description: Download page advantage description, emphasizing unified distribution

message.mobileUnifiedExperience.message: The account system, quota, and service entry after logging in are consistent with the web version, making it easy for users to switch seamlessly between desktop and mobile.
message.mobileUnifiedExperience.description: Download page advantage description, emphasizing unified account experience

message.remainingAmount.description: Used to display the remaining amount of service

message.copied.description: Message indicating that the content has been successfully copied

message.restart.description: Message indicating that the content has been successfully copied

message.confirmDelete.message: Are you sure you want to delete?
message.confirmDelete.description: Message confirming that the user wants to delete an item

message.expiredAt.description: Message displaying the expiration time of the service

message.deleteAccountConfirm.message: This will permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone. Are you sure you want to continue?
message.deleteAccountConfirm.description: Confirmation message shown before permanently deleting the user account

message.deleteAccountSuccess.description: Message shown after the user account is successfully deleted

message.deleteAccountFailed.message: Failed to delete your account. Please try again later.
message.deleteAccountFailed.description: Message shown when account deletion fails

message.free.description: Message indicating that the service is free, meaning no payment is required

message.startPrice.description: Message displaying the minimum price of the service

message.joinDiscord.description: Message inviting the user to join the Discord channel

message.addWeChat.description: Message inviting the user to add a WeChat account

message.noData.description: Message indicating that there is no data in the table or list

customers.comment1.message: Ace Data Cloud has provided great service to our team, helping us improve productivity. We love it!

customers.comment2.message: Ace Data Cloud is a great integration platform with excellent service quality.

customers.comment3.message: I love the service from Ace Data Cloud, it's user-friendly and reasonably priced. I hope it keeps getting better!

settings.github.description: Link to view the source code on GitHub

settings.about.description: Title for the about section in the settings page

settings.localTools.description: Desktop-only settings tab for managing local tool folders, permissions and grants.

settings.localToolsDesktopOnly.message: Available in the desktop app only.
settings.localToolsDesktopOnly.description: Shown when the Local Tools tab is opened on web/mobile.

settings.localToolsFoldersTitle.description: Heading for the list of folders the AI may read/write locally.

settings.localToolsFoldersHint.message: Folders the AI may read and write on this machine. Tools run locally; nothing leaves your device without your consent.
settings.localToolsFoldersHint.description: Help text under the authorized-folders heading.

settings.localToolsComputerUseTitle.description: Heading for the opt-in Computer Use (screen + mouse/keyboard control) toggle.

settings.localToolsComputerUseHint.message: Let the AI see your screen and control the mouse and keyboard on this computer. Off by default — turn it on only when you need it. On macOS, grant Screen Recording and Accessibility permissions below. Each action still asks for your confirmation.
settings.localToolsComputerUseHint.description: Help text under the Computer Use toggle.

settings.localToolsPreauthorizeHint: Allow screenshot, click, type, key and every computer action at once, and trigger the required system permissions.
settings.localToolsCuActionsHint: Turn on only the actions you're comfortable letting the AI run without asking each time. Each stays revocable here.
settings.localToolsBuiltinHint: Turn on a tool to let the AI run it without asking each time. Shell can run any command on this machine — enable only if you trust it. File tools stay limited to your authorized folders.

settings.localToolsAddFolder.description: Button to pick a folder to authorize.

settings.localToolsRemove.description: Button to remove an authorized folder.

settings.localToolsNoFolders.description: Empty state for the authorized-folders list.

settings.localToolsMcpTitle.description: Section title for local MCP server configuration in Local Tools settings.

settings.localToolsMcpHint.message: Connect local MCP servers over stdio. Their tools become available to the AI, gated by the same per-call confirmation. Changes take effect immediately.
settings.localToolsMcpHint.description: Explains what local MCP servers do and that changes apply without a restart.

settings.localToolsMcpAdd.description: Button to add a new local MCP server row.

settings.localToolsMcpName.description: Label for the MCP server name/id field.

settings.localToolsMcpCommand.description: Label for the MCP server launch command field.

settings.localToolsMcpArgs.description: Label for the MCP server command arguments field.

settings.localToolsMcpArgsHint.description: Placeholder/hint for the MCP arguments textarea.

settings.localToolsMcpEnv.description: Label for the MCP server environment variables field.

settings.localToolsMcpEnvHint.description: Placeholder/hint for the MCP environment variables textarea.

settings.localToolsMcpNoServers.description: Empty state shown when no MCP servers are configured.

settings.localToolsMcpNamePlaceholder.description: Placeholder for the MCP server name input.

settings.localToolsMcpCommandPlaceholder.description: Placeholder for the MCP server command input.

settings.localToolsMcpNameRequired.message: Each server needs a name and a command.
settings.localToolsMcpNameRequired.description: Validation error when a server row is missing a name or command.

settings.localToolsMcpDuplicateName.description: Validation error when two servers share the same name.

settings.localToolsMcpNameInvalid.message: Name may only contain letters, numbers, hyphens and underscores.
settings.localToolsMcpNameInvalid.description: Validation error when a server name contains invalid characters.

settings.localToolsMcpEnabled.description: Toggle label to enable/disable a local MCP server.

settings.localToolsMcpReconnect.description: Button to re-spawn one MCP server and refresh its status.

settings.localToolsMcpConnecting.description: Badge while an MCP server is being (re)connected.

settings.localToolsMcpNotSaved.description: Badge for an MCP server row that hasn't been saved/connected yet.

settings.localToolsMcpConnected.description: Badge when an MCP server connected; {n} is the tool count.

settings.localToolsMcpDisabled.description: Badge when an MCP server is turned off.

settings.localToolsMcpFailed.description: Badge when an MCP server failed to start; the reason is shown below.

settings.localToolsMcpPlatformHint.message: Tip: use a command on your PATH such as npx or uvx (e.g. npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem ~/Documents). On Windows, npx/node resolve automatically. If a server shows "spawn … ENOENT", set the command to a full path.
settings.localToolsMcpPlatformHint.description: Cross-platform hint for configuring the MCP launch command.

settings.localToolsSaved.description: Transient confirmation after saving local-tool folders.

settings.localToolsActiveTools.description: Label before the list of currently available local tools.

settings.localToolsGrantsTitle.description: Heading for the persistent consent grants list.

settings.localToolsGrantsHint.message: Tool calls you chose to always allow. They run without asking again. Revoke any to re-arm the confirmation prompt.
settings.localToolsGrantsHint.description: Help text under the always-allowed actions heading.

settings.localToolsRevoke.description: Button to revoke a single always-allow grant.

settings.localToolsRevokeAll.description: Button to revoke every always-allow grant.

settings.localToolsNoGrants.message: No always-allowed actions. You'll be asked before each local tool runs.
settings.localToolsNoGrants.description: Empty state for the always-allowed actions list.

settings.localToolsPermsTitle.description: Heading for the macOS system-permission section.

settings.localToolsPermsHint.message: Optional. Folder access above needs none of these — grant only what a specific task requires.
settings.localToolsPermsHint.description: Help text under the system-permissions heading.

settings.localToolsPermFullDisk.description: macOS Full Disk Access permission row.

settings.localToolsGranted.description: Status tag when a system permission is granted.

settings.localToolsNotGranted.description: Status tag when a system permission is not granted.

settings.localToolsOpen.description: Button to open the relevant macOS System Settings pane.

settings.subsites.description: Title for the subsites management tab in the settings popup. Visible only on the official main site; subsites are not displayed.

settings.adminOnlyHint.message: Only site administrators can see this section. Regular users do not have access.
settings.adminOnlyHint.description: Hint shown at the top of admin-only settings tabs (Site / SEO / Distribution / Function).

settings.officialOnlyHint.message: Only the official main site can see this section. Subsites do not show this entry.
settings.officialOnlyHint.description: Hint shown at the top of the My Subsites tab, explaining the entry only appears on the official main site.

settings.customDomain.description: Title for the custom-domain management tab in the settings popup. Visible only on subsites (non-main-official hosts) for site admins.

settings.buildNow.description: Text for the 'Build Subsite' button in the about page, visible only on the official main site

settings.poweredBy.description: Title displaying which open-source project this site is built on in the about page

settings.poweredByTip.message: This site is built on the Nexior open-source project, with fully open-source code.
settings.poweredByTip.description: Description in the about page showing which open-source project this site is based on

settings.apiService.description: Title displaying the API service provider in the about page

settings.apiServiceTip.message: The API service for this site is provided by Ace Data Cloud.
settings.apiServiceTip.description: Description in the about page showing the API service provider

settings.buildYourOwn.description: Title prompting users that they can build a similar site in the about page

settings.buildYourOwnTip.message: Want to build a similar AI site? Contact us for deployment support.
settings.buildYourOwnTip.description: Description in the about page for building a similar site

settings.contactUs.description: Text for the contact support button

message.viewOnGithub.description: Link text to view the project on GitHub

message.notFound.message: The page you are looking for does not exist. The link may have changed or been removed. Return to the homepage to continue exploring AceData's AI services.

message.notFoundEyebrow.description: Text for the small label at the top of the 404 page

message.notFoundPath.description: Label displaying the current requested path on the 404 page

button.backToHome.description: Button text for navigating back to the homepage

button.goBack.description: Button text for returning to the previous page in browsing history

title.apiCode.description: Title of the dialog that shows the equivalent API code (curl / Python / Node) that would reproduce a result generated inside the product

message.viewCodeHint.message: Show API code for this request
message.viewCodeHint.description: Tooltip text on the View Code button shown alongside result-card action buttons (U1/U2/Download/Extend/...). Clicking the button opens a dialog with curl / Python / Node snippets that reproduce the same generation via the public API.

button.viewCode.description: Button text that opens a dialog with the equivalent API code (curl / Python / Node) for the current result, similar to the OpenAI Playground 'View code' affordance

button.getApiKey.description: Link text in the API code dialog footer that opens the platform console page where users can create an API key

button.apiPlatform.description: API code dialog footer button label. Clicking opens https://platform.acedata.cloud/ in a new tab so users can manage their API keys, balance and usage.

message.apiCodeIntro.message: Run this from your own code to produce the same result.
message.apiCodeIntro.description: API code dialog: short hint shown above the language tabs. Explains that the snippet below reproduces the current result when run from the user's own code.

tab.apiRequest.description: API code dialog tab label for the request snippet (the code that reproduces the current result).

tab.apiResponse.description: API code dialog tab label for the live response returned after clicking Run.

button.apiRun.description: API code dialog button that re-runs the request live against the API using the user's key. The result already exists, so clicking runs it again, and it is billable.

button.apiRunning.description: API code dialog Run button label while the live request is in flight.

button.apiRunConfirm.description: Confirm-button label in the dialog that warns running here consumes extra credits.

button.viewDocs.description: API code dialog footer button that opens the matching API documentation page on the platform.

message.apiClickRun.message: Click Run again to execute this request live with your API key.
message.apiClickRun.description: API code dialog Response tab empty state, shown before the user has run the request.

message.apiRunConfirmTitle.description: Title of the confirmation dialog shown before re-running a request from the API code dialog.

message.apiRunConfirmDesc.message: Running here makes a real API call with your API key and consumes extra credits. Continue?
message.apiRunConfirmDesc.description: Body of the confirmation dialog warning that re-running the request is a real, billable API call.

message.apiRunConfirmSkip.description: Checkbox in the run-confirmation dialog that, when ticked, suppresses the warning on subsequent runs.

message.apiRunTaskCreated.message: Task created — close this window to see the latest result below.
message.apiRunTaskCreated.description: Success banner shown in the API code dialog after a run returns a task id, hinting that the new task already appears in the list behind the dialog.

button.apiViewLatest.description: Button in the API code dialog success banner that closes the dialog so the user can see the newly created task in the list below.

message.uploadInProgress.message: Reference uploads are still in progress — please wait a moment.
message.uploadInProgress.description: Toast warning shown when the user clicks the Generate button while reference image / video / audio uploads are still in flight. Tells them to wait for the upload to finish before re-clicking Generate.

appUpgrade.title.description: Title of the native-app version gate modal shown at startup when the installed build is older than what the backend supports.

appUpgrade.blockMessage.message: This version is no longer supported. Please update from the app store to continue.
appUpgrade.blockMessage.description: Body text of the hard-block upgrade modal shown when the installed version is below min_supported. The modal has no dismiss affordance — the only button opens the app store.

appUpgrade.softMessage.message: A newer version is available. Update from the app store for the best experience.
appUpgrade.softMessage.description: Body text of the soft upgrade prompt shown when the installed version is below latest but above min_supported. The user can dismiss and keep using the app.

appUpgrade.openStore.description: Primary button on the upgrade modal — opens the App Store / Play Store URL in the system browser.

appUpgrade.later.description: Secondary button on the soft upgrade prompt — dismisses the modal and lets the user keep using the current version. Only shown for non-forced upgrades.

button.deletePermanently.description: Final red delete button at the bottom of the delete confirmation dialog

message.deleteAccountWarning.message: This action is irreversible! Your account and all of its data will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.
message.deleteAccountWarning.description: Red warning banner at the top of the delete confirmation dialog

message.deleteAccountConsequences.message: Permanently deleted: profile, balance and credits, applications and subscriptions, API keys, and third-party login bindings.
message.deleteAccountConsequences.description: Explains what data will be removed in the delete confirmation dialog

message.deleteAccountTypePrompt.message: Type your username to confirm deletion:
message.deleteAccountTypePrompt.description: Instructs the user to type their username to confirm; the username is shown separately below

message.deleteAccountPlaceholder.description: Placeholder for the username confirmation input

nav.maestro.description: Text in the website navigation bar for the Maestro article-to-video page, keep it as 'Maestro'

nav.digitalhuman.description: Text in the website navigation bar for the Digital Human talking-head page

settings.localToolsAndroidPermHint.message: Enable the accessibility service so the assistant can see and control your screen. You can pre-authorize individual actions below.
settings.localToolsAndroidPermHint.description: Hint for the Android Computer Use accessibility permission section in Settings.

settings.cuConsentTitle.description: Title of the on-demand consent dialog shown when the assistant requests a screen action.

settings.cuConsentMessage.message: The assistant wants to run "{action}" on your phone. Allow it?
settings.cuConsentMessage.description: Body of the on-demand consent dialog; {action} is the computer.* action name.

settings.cuConsentAllow.description: Allow button of the on-demand consent dialog.

settings.cuConsentAlways.description: Always-allow button of the on-demand consent dialog; persists a grant so this action never prompts again.

settings.cuConsentDeny.description: Deny button of the on-demand consent dialog.
